verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is a member of the EGF family synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane precursor (~46 kDa) that is proteolytically processed by sheddases (ADAM10/17) to release a soluble ~17 kDa mature peptide. The TM-anchored proform is the obligate precursor and can engage EGFR/ERBB4 in juxtacrine signaling before cleavage. The dominant biological actor in most contexts is the shed soluble ligand (detected in serum and conditioned media), making the TM precursor stage transient rather than stable. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern → contextual/dual_localization. The precursor does transiently reside at the outer leaflet and can mediate juxtacrine activity there, but clinical antibody programs predominantly target the shed soluble form.
